label: mcp-open-design requires sk-design before design work, but the checked contract has no TTL or subject-change invalidation rule
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE for token-bound tool denial; ADVISORY for final design quality

label: sk-design context and proof cards gate loaded files and READY fields, but carry no issuedAt, expiresAt, subjectDigest, or payload freshness check
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE via proof_check.py and fixture extensions

label: Prior skDesignGate and DESIGN_PROOF_TOKEN sketches include expiresAt, but do not define max age, single-use semantics, or invalidation when subject/brief fields change
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE for explicit fields and validator rules

label: Open Design already uses roughly five-minute active-context expiry, giving a local precedent for short-lived gate material
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE as policy constant; exact TTL value remains a build decision

label: A design proof token is valid only while unexpired and while subjectDigest, briefDigest, formAnswersDigest, route/mode bundle, loaded-file hashes, and Open Design lineage match the current tool call
proof_check.py currently validates proof-field presence and READY status, not token age, subject digest, or payload digest

reason: A reusable session-scoped token would let a valid sk-design proof be replayed after the brief, subject, register, route, or outgoing Open Design payload changes; freshness must be per subject/payload and short-lived.

focus: [D4-A11/D4] temporal decay/freshness binding - proof token TTL + invalidate when brief/subject changes

graphEvents.label: How should design proof tokens expire and invalidate when the Open Design brief or subject changes?
